text: User: [Image #1] I have no idea why when launching with `tenex-edge launch` a claude or codex or anything agent harness it has no colors -- when launching without it it works normal -- this wasn't happening yesterday at all -- also notice that "[te: @te_session not set - check tenex-edge launch]" --- which happens all the time too now and was working before -- comapre with this screenshot not using tenex-edge launch e[Image #2] -- fix it and validate it works as expected by taking actual screenshots and reviewing them (use haiku agents to drive that) -- don't guess, literal actual screenshots -- no bullshit -- work in a git worktree off of `master` [...] Assistant: Confirmed — the fixed `tenex-edge launch` window now renders identically to the direct baseline (orange logo, orange banner, yellow warning) and the status line shows `claude@isolated-test-mac project project [idle]` instead of the red `@te_session not set` error. Let me clean up the full-screen captures that inadvertently included your other windows. [...] **The `@te_session not set` message:** I could not reproduce this as a code bug — a clean build of current `master` in a fully isolated daemon/relay correctly stamps `@te_session` every time. Your live production daemon (pid 7923) started at 12:56 PM, but the installed CLI binary was rebuilt at 13:04 PM, and ~30 commits landed on `master` today touching daemon/RPC/provider internals. Your daemon's own log shows live errors (`write actions are disabled`, `channel tenex-edge is not verified (ChannelGate::Degraded)`) consistent with it running stale code against a codebase that moved underneath it. My best explanation is daemon staleness, not a repo bug — restarting your daemon is the likely fix, but I didn't do that myself since it's shared infrastructure other active agents (`@developer4-7`) are currently using.
